NOTICE:   MASKS ARE STRONGLY ENCOURAGED!!
***REGISTER TO VOTE HERE!!***
https://registertovote.ca.gov/
The last day to register to vote for the June 2, 2026, Primary Election is May 18, 2026
The last day to register to vote for the November 3, 2026, General Election is October 19, 2026
Link to City Council Agendas/Packets
http://www.ci.richmond.ca.us/4157/City-ofRichmond-Council-Meetings
Register to receive notification of new agendas, etc.
http://www.ci.richmond.ca.us/list.aspx
HOW TO WATCH THE MEETING FROM HOME:
KCRT – Comcast Channel 28 or AT&T Uverse Channel 99
Livestream online at
http://www.ci.richmond.ca.us/3178/KCRT-Live
HOW TO SUBMIT PUBLIC COMMENTS:
In Person:
Anyone who desires to address the City Council during
Open Forum
, must complete and file a pink speaker’s card with the City Clerk
prior
to the City Council’s consideration of the item.
Online speakers:
must raise their hand when instructed by the clerk. Once the City Clerk has announced the item and given a count of the number of total speakers, no person shall be permitted to speak on the item other than those persons who have submitted their names to the City Clerk or those that have their hand raised.
The amount of time allotted to individual speakers shall be determined based on the total number of persons requesting to speak during this item. The time allocation for each speaker will be as follows: 15 or fewer speakers, a maximum of 2 minutes; 16 to 24 speakers, a maximum of 1 and one-half minutes; and 25 or more speakers, a maximum of 1 minute.
NON-PUBLIC HEARING ITEMS LISTED ON THE AGENDA: Each speaker will be allowed up to TWO minutes to address the Council.  PUBLIC HEARING ITEMS: Speakers are allowed up to THREE (3) minutes to address the Council.
Via Zoom
:
Anyone who desires to address the City Council on items appearing on the agenda,
including Open Forum
, must raise their hand once public comment is open.
The public speakers attending in person will be called first, followed by Zoom participants (when appropriate).  Only those public speakers that have their hand raised at the appropriate time will be recognized.
Open Session and City Council:
Please click the link below to join the webinar:
https://zoom.us/j/99312205643?pwd=MDdqNnRmS2k4ZkRTOWhlUldQOUF1Zz09
Passcode: ccmeeting
By iPhone one-tap
:  US: +16699006833,,99312205643#  or +13462487799,,99312205643#
By Telephone
:   Dial (for higher quality, dial a number based on your current location):  US: +1 669 900 6833 or +1 346 248 7799 or +1 253 215 8782 or +1 312 626 6799 or +1 929 205 6099 or +1 301 715 8592
Webinar ID: 993 1220 5643
International numbers available:
https://zoom.us/u/aehrwCgISx
To comment by video conference:
click on the Participants button at the bottom of your screen and select the “
Raise Your Hand
” button to request to speak when Public Comment is being asked for.
When called upon, press the unmute button. After the allotted time, you will then be re-muted.  **
To comment by phone:
you will be prompted to “Raise Your Hand” by pressing “
*9
” to request to speak when Public Comment is asked for.
When called upon, you will be asked to unmuted by pressing *6.
After the allotted time, you will then be re-muted. Instructions of how to raise your hand by phone are available at:
https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/201362663 -Joining-a-meeting-by-phone.
**
**
The mayor will announce the agenda item number and open public comment when appropriate.
Via mail:
received by 1:00 p.m. the day of the meeting, sent to 450 Civic Center Plaza, 3rd Floor, Office of the Clerk, Richmond, CA 94804.
Via eComments
: eComments are available once an agenda is published. Locate the meeting in "upcoming meetings" and click the comment bubble icon. Click on the item you wish to comment on. eComments can be submitted when the agenda is published and until the conclusion of public comments for the agenda item. Email your comment to
[email protected]
should you have difficulty submitting an eComment during a meeting.
Via email:
to
[email protected]
by 1:00 p.m
. the day of the meeting.
Emails
MUST
contain in the subject line 1) public comments – Open Session prior to Closed Session; 2) public comments – Open Forum; or 3) public comments agenda item #__ [include the agenda item number].
Properly labeled public comments
will be considered a public record, put into the official meeting record, available after the meeting as supplemental materials, and will be posted as an attachment to the meeting minutes when the minutes are posted:
http://www.ci.richmond.ca.us/Archive.aspx?AMID=31
The City cannot guarantee that its network and/or the site will be uninterrupted. To ensure that the City Council receives your comments, you are strongly encouraged to submit your comments in writing in advance of the meeting.
Procedures for Removing Items from the Consent Calendar
:
Members of the public who request to remove an item from the consent calendar must first discuss the item with a staff member by phone or a back-and-forth email discussion, and state the name of the staff member when requesting removal of the item from the consent calendar
(by 2 p.m. the day of the meeting)
.  The procedures for discussion do not apply to items sponsored by the mayor or councilmembers.
Any member of the City Council who would like to remove an item from the consent calendar must notify the appropriate staff person and the City Clerk’s Office prior to the meeting. Although members of the City Council are encouraged to ask questions and share concerns with staff ahead of the meeting, they are not required to do so.
The Clerk’s Office must be informed of any requests to remove items from the Consent Calendar. Items removed from the Consent Calendar shall be placed at the end of the agenda for consideration.
CONDUCT AT MEETINGS:
Richmond City Council meetings are limited public forums during which the City strives to provide an open, safe atmosphere and promote robust public debate. Members of the public, however, must comply with state law, as well as the City’s laws and procedures and may not actually disrupt the orderly conduct of these meetings. The public, for example, may not shout or use amplifying devices, must submit comment cards and speak during their allotted time, may not create a physical disturbance, may not speak on matters unrelated to issues within the jurisdiction of the City Council or the agenda item at hand, and may not cause immediate threats to public safety.
Click here for City Harassment Policy
Accessibility for Individuals with Disabilities:
Upon request, the City will provide for written agenda materials in appropriate alternative formats, or disability-related modification or accommodation, including auxiliary aids or services and sign language interpreters, to enable individuals with disabilities to participate in and provide comments at/related to public meetings. Please submit a request, including your name, phone number and/or email address, and a description of the modification, accommodation, auxiliary aid, service or alternative format requested at least two days before the meeting.  Requests should be emailed to
[email protected]
or submitted by phone at 510-620-6513, ext. 9, or 510-620-6509.  Requests made by mail to City Clerk’s Office, City Council meeting, 450 Civic Center Plaza, Richmond, CA 94804 must be received at least two days before the meeting. Requests will be granted whenever possible and resolved in favor of accessibility.
Unless otherwise noted in an agenda description, all actions taken by the Council include a determination that CEQA does not apply (i.e., the action is not a project and therefore exempt from CEQA).
Disclaimer:  The City Clerk's Office translates agendas to enhance public access.  Translated agendas are provided as a courtesy and do not constitute legal notice of the meeting or items to be discussed.  In case of discrepancy, the English agenda will prevail.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $300,000 grant to Hope Solutions for Tiny House Village
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $300,000 grant agreement with Hope Solutions to fund a Tiny House Village and Garden Project at 175 23rd Street. The grant covers $103,000 in capital costs (three-unit restroom trailer, deck materials, yurt) and $197,000 for operating costs, paid on a reimbursement basis. The village will have 7 tiny houses serving 6 unhoused youth ages 18-24 plus one resident manager unit. The agreement term is April 1, 2026 through December 31, 2027. Total development costs are estimated at $978,912, with TVS responsible for raising approximately $875,000.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this project could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.2.a
Grant Agreement with Contra Costa Interfaith Transitional Housing, Inc. (dba Hope Solutions)
APPROVE a grant agreement in an amount not to exceed $300,000 with Contra Costa Interfaith Transitional Housing, Inc. (dba Hope Solutions) to provide capital and supportive services funding for the Tiny House Village and Garden Project at 175 23rd Street for a term expiring December 31, 2027– Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6649).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on grant application for 59-unit affordable housing
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council will vote on authorizing the city to participate as a non-Applicant in an AHSC grant application for the Riveter Project at 100 38th Street. The grant seeks up to $35M total, with ~$22M as a loan for affordable housing and ~$10M as a grant for bike/pedestrian improvements and three new zero-emission AC Transit buses. The project includes 59 affordable housing units (50 supportive housing) via adaptive reuse of a former courthouse.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision — if approved, the city manager may execute agreements for bike/pedestrian improvements and grant fund appropriation.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.2.b
Authorize AHSC Grant Application for Riveter Project and Associated Active Transportation Improvements
ADOPT a resolution to AUTHORIZE the City’s participation in an Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities Program (AHSC) grant application for the Riveter Project, a 59-unit affordable housing development at 100 38th Street, for the purposes of implementing certain pedestrian and bicycle improvements, and AUTOHRIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the necessary agreements with the Co-Applicants to complete the project components with grant funds and appropriate the grant funds, if awarded – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco 510-620-6841).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on MOU with RecycleMore for SB 1383 waste rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with RecycleMore to implement SB 1383. The MOU designates RecycleMore to handle education, reporting, edible food recovery, complaint investigation, inspection, enforcement, and waiver policy. The City retains responsibility for enforcement, waivers, and procurement. Funding comes from existing solid waste rates and franchise fees. CalRecycle expects to audit Richmond for compliance in 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, your business may need to comply with new organic waste sorting and collection rules under SB 1383.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.3.a
Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the West Contra Costa Integrated Waste Management Authority (RecycleMore) for Implementation of California Senate Bill 1383 (SB 1383)
ADOPT a resolution approving a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the City and the West Contra Costa Integrated Waste Management Authority (RecycleMore) regarding implementation of California Senate Bill 1383 (SB 1383) regulatory requirements and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the MOU – Economic Development Department (Samantha Carr 510-620-5407).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $112,900 contract for Port of Richmond economic study
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $112,900 contract with John C. Martin Associates, LLC to conduct an economic impact study of the Port of Richmond. The study would analyze how port activity affects local jobs, business revenue, and the broader economy. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you now — watch for the council decision, as the study's findings could affect port-area businesses.
agenda_briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Construction & Development, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Contract with John C. Martin Associates, LLC for the Port Economic Impact Study
APPROVE a contract with John C. Martin Associates, LLC to provide an economic impact study of Port of Richmond activity, in an amount not to exceed $112,900, with a term ending June 30, 2027 - Economic Development Department (Charles Gerard 510-620-6792).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $29,000 software contract amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed $29,000 amendment to an existing contract with LCPtracker, Inc. If approved, this would extend the company's labor compliance software services through March 4, 2027, for a total contract value not to exceed $56,999. The software is used to track certified payroll, workforce data, and ensure compliance with the City's Local Employment Program Ordinance and Business Opportunity Ordinance on City-funded projects. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at an upcoming Council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed LCPtracker contract amendment vote — Richmond Council

agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.4.a
Contract Amendment No. 2 with LCP Tracker for Labor Compliance Monitoring and Reporting Services
APPROVE the second amendment to the agreement with LCPtracker, Inc., in the amount of $29,000, for a total contract amount not to exceed $56,999, for the term beginning March 5, 2025, and ending March 4, 2027, to continue providing labor compliance software for certified payroll, workforce tracking, and ordinance compliance on City-funded projects – Community Services Department/Employment and Training (Tamara Walker 510-307-8006/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $42,000 sole-source contract for interlibrary courier services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving a sole-source contract with Unity Courier Service, Inc. for Link+ interlibrary loan courier services. A sole-source contract means the city plans to hire this one company without bidding it out to others. The proposed contract would run from July 1, 2025 to June 30, 2028, with a total payment limit of $42,000 ($14,000 per year). This decision has not been finalized yet.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — interlibrary courier services

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 amendment for electric bikeshare program operations
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility that would add $390,000 to the city's electric bikeshare program, bringing the total contract to $3,540,341. The extra money would keep the program running until the end of the fiscal year while Council decides the program's long-term future during the 2026-27 budget process. The funds would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.4.c
​​Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations​
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to accept $60,000 California Tahoe Conservancy grant for youth outdoor programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to accept and appropriate $60,000 in grant funds from the California Tahoe Conservancy's 'Tahoe for All' Grant Program. If approved, the money would go to the Community Services Department/Recreation to support the Pathfinders: Youth Outdoors Richmond Weekend Camping Program, which provides free, three-day camping experiences at Sugar Pine Point State Park for Richmond youth. The program will run over three years, with two camping trips annually serving 15 youth each (90 total), plus 4-6 pre-trip activities per year for 40-60 additional youth. All costs (transportation, meals, equipment, instruction) are fully covered. The city manager or their designee would also be authorized to sign the grant agreement. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.4.d
Accept and Appropriate California Tahoe Conservancy -Tahoe for All Grant Funds
ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE grant funds in the amount of $60,000 from California Tahoe Conservancy-Tahoe for All Grant Program Fund; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the agreement – Community Services Department/Recreation (Ranjana Maharaj/Jene Levine- Snipes 510-307-8132).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $3.275M amendment for Harbour-8 Park expansion and operations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a fourth contract amendment with Pogo Park to complete the Harbour-8 Park Expansion Project. This would increase the payment limit by $3,275,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $12,430,725. Up to $1,000,000 will repay a REDF Impact Investing Fund bridge loan. Council will also consider authorizing an Operations and Management Agreement with Pogo Park for the programming and maintenance of park facilities, with a term ending May 1, 2029, and two one-year extension options. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.

key_dates.label: Proposed Harbour-8 Park operations agreement term ends

agenda_briefs.source_body:

Contract Amendment No. 4 with Pogo Park for the Harbour-8 Park Expansion Project and Operating and Management

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond-Sebastia Sister City Commission to be established
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution to create a new Richmond-Sebastia Sister City Commission. If approved, the commission would have 5–11 members (at least 70% must be Richmond residents), serve 3-year terms with no term limits, and be directed to write its own bylaws subject to final City Council approval. The commission's role is advisory only and cannot financially obligate the city without council approval.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.5.d
Establish the Richmond-Sebastia Sister City Commission
ADOPT a resolution establishing the Richmond-Sebastia Sister City Commission, directing the Commission to prepare bylaws subject to City Council approval, and establishing basic organizational requirements - Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-5303).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $250,000 contract for wastewater operator advisor services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $250,000 contract with The Halleman Group, LLC to help the city find and hire a private operator for its wastewater treatment plant, wastewater collection system, and stormwater collection system. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the contract would run from May 1, 2026 to April 30, 2028. The city manager would be authorized to sign the paperwork. The existing operator contract with Veolia expires May 15, 2027.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for limited-term revenue study
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider approving a contract with Dalberg Advisors to help the city explore new, temporary ways to raise money (called 'limited-term revenues'). This could mean new fees or charges for businesses or residents in the future. Council has not made a final decision yet — this is just the first step to hire experts to study the options. The public engagement update shows the city has been gathering community input on what these new revenues could look like.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this study could lead to new fees or charges that affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive presentation on BART service and fiscal crisis
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a presentation from BART Director Barnali Ghosh on BART's fiscal crisis caused by remote work. BART faces a structural operating deficit of more than $370 million annually beginning in Fiscal Year 2027. A regional transportation funding measure (SB 63) will appear on the November 2026 ballot; if approved, it would provide BART with $310 million annually starting in FY28. If the measure fails, BART's Alternative Service Plan would take effect in January 2027, including a 63% service reduction, 9 p.m. closure, 30% fare and parking increases, and over 600 layoffs, with further cuts in July 2027 potentially closing up to 15 stations.

key_dates.label: November 2026 ballot measure on regional transit funding
BART Alternative Service Plan cuts take effect if measure fails
Potential further BART service cuts if measure fails

agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Presentation from San Francisco Bay Bay Area Rapid Transit District (BART) Director Barnali Ghosh for 2026
RECEIVE a presentation from the San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District (BART) Director regarding BART’s recent service improvements and the transit fiscal crisis due to Work From Home – City Manager’s Office (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the rules for sidewalk vendors in Richmond. The changes would update how vendors apply for permits, how waivers are handled, and how the city enforces the rules. This is the first reading of the ordinance — it has not been approved yet. If passed, it would change Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (the city's local law book). The Public Works Department is leading this proposal.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, these changes could affect your sidewalk vending permit or application process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477).

attachments.title: Attachment 1 - Bid Summary Owner Advisors v2.pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $299,797 contract for Red Oak Victory relocation planning
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider approving a $299,797 contract with Liftech Consultants Inc. for planning and preliminary design work to relocate the SS Red Oak Victory ship. The contract would run until June 30, 2027. The work includes an existing conditions assessment, cost analysis and value engineering, a permitting pathway analysis, and preliminary engineering to the 15% design level to support CEQA compliance. The total estimated cost to design, permit, and construct the relocation is $16 million to $20 million (2027 dollars). This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be discussed at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.3
Contract with Liftech Consultants, Inc. for Planning and Design Services for the Relocation of the SS Red Oak Victory
RECEIVE a presentation from Rosie the Riveter / WWII Home Front National Historical Park and APPROVE a contract with Liftech Consultants Inc. in the amount of $299,797 for planning and preliminary design of the relocation of the Red Oak for a term ending June 30, 2027 - Economic Development Department/Port (Charles Gerard 510-620-6792).

Creating a Park and Playground in The Pullman Neighborhood .pdf

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore park acquisition in Pullman neighborhood
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to direct staff to work with the Pullman Neighborhood Council on acquiring a vacant lot (APN 513-025-001-1, 7,000 sq ft) at South 27th Street and Florida Avenue. The goal is to evaluate feasibility, find funding, and develop a plan to create a new park and playground for Pullman families, addressing years of underinvestment in this underserved area. Staff would be asked to return to Council with recommendations and a timeline within 90 days. This is a proposed step — no decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect local property values and community development in the Pullman area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.4
Creating a Park and Playground in Pullman Neighborhood to begin to Correct Decades of Systemic Underinvestment in this Underserved Neighborhood
DIRECT staff to work with the Pullman Neighborhood Council to explore acquisition of Parcel # (APN): 513-025-001-1 comprising the vacant lot at South 27th Street and Florida Avenue to evaluate feasibility, funding opportunities, and development pathways to establish and develop a neighborhood park that serves Pullman families and to RETURN to the City Council with recommendations and a proposed timeline within 90 days - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568) and Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on opposing CO2 pipeline projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution that would formally oppose the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond and other parts of Contra Costa and Solano counties to transport and store carbon waste underground. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez has proposed the resolution, citing risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents from potential pipeline leaks or accidents. The vote has not yet happened, and the resolution is up for discussion and approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez at 510-620-6565 to express your support or concerns before the council vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.5
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive presentation on earthquake preparedness
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from UC Berkeley Seismology Lab, Berkeley Center for Smart Infrastructure, CERT, Em Comm, and West Contra Costa Fire Safe Council on earthquake preparedness. The presentation covers a 15% probability of a M6.7+ earthquake on the Hayward Fault before 2044, with a predicted M7.1 event causing MMI 8 shaking at 10 km distance. The city is 157 years past the last major Hayward Fault earthquake (1868). No decision or bylaw is proposed; future policy recommendations requiring funding will return for fiscal evaluation in fall 2026.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.6
Presentation by UC Berkeley Scientists and Community Partners on Earthquake Preparedness
RECEIVE a presentation from leading experts—the UC Berkeley Seismology Lab, Berkeley Center for Smart Infrastructure, Community Emergency Response Team (CERT), Emergency Communication (Em Comm), and West Contra Costa Fire Safe Council (WCCFSC)—regarding local earthquake probabilities, regional vulnerabilities - Councilmember Soheila Bana (510-672-3490).
